== Notes for [[Item:Q108539|Q108539]] ==

* '''Former Slave Girl, From Yemen'''

'''al-Khayzuran''' and her strong personality is believed by many literary historians to be a key influence on Scheherazade, the main character in 1001 Nights. Many of the stories were influenced by the 5th Abbasid Caliph [http://www.geni.com/people/Caliph-H%C4%81r%C5%ABn-ar-Rash%C4%ABd/6000000010760854680/ Hārūn ar-Rashīd] and his fabulous court.

Source 1: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al-Khayzuran

Source 2: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abassa

Source 3: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zubaidah_bint_Ja%60far
--------------------
Al-Khayzuran bint Atta (Arabic: الخيزران بنت عطاء‎) (died 789) was the wife of the Abbasid Caliph Al-Mahdi and mother and co-regent of both Caliphs Al-Hadi and Harun al-Rashid.

Al-Khayzuran was from Jorash, near modern Bisha, Saudi Arabia. She was kidnapped from her home by a Bedouin who then sold her in a slave market near Mecca to Al-Mahdi during his pilgrimage. ref: https://www.wikiwand.com/en/Al-Khayzuran

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000014686765396

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
